title: Repair master_data schema USAGE grant on production + fix migration filename generator

incident.symptom: permission denied for schema master_data → permit/toll-tag CRUD routes failing in production
incident.root_cause: PR #684 hotfix shipped as db/migrations/20260607_183150_grant_master_data_schema_to_app.sql. Its YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS filename (8 digits, underscore, 6 digits) matched NEITHER runner pattern in scripts/db-migrate.mjs (legacy /^\d{4}[a-z]?_/ nor timestamp /^\d{12}_/), so listMigrationFiles() filtered it out. db:migrate silently skipped it: never applied, never ledgered, no error. The file existed on origin/main and deployed, but the GRANT never ran.
incident.secondary_root_cause: generateMigrationName() emitted the same unrunnable YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS format, so any migration scaffolded with the helper would be silently skipped.

fix.part_1: New idempotent migration db/migrations/202606072230_grant_master_data_schema_to_app.sql (runner-valid 12-digit YYYYMMDDHHMM prefix) granting USAGE + table/sequence grants + ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES on schema master_data to ih35_app.
fix.part_2: Fixed generateMigrationName() to emit YYYYMMDDHHMM_<slug>.sql (12 continuous digits) matching MIGRATION_FILE_PATTERN_TIMESTAMP. Deleted the stale invalid file 20260607_183150_grant_master_data_schema_to_app.sql (can never apply). Corrected docs/runbooks/migration-naming.md examples.

ci_gates.schema-grant-check: PASS — master_data (created in 0407) has GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA ... TO ih35_app supplied by the new co-committed migration
ci_gates.migration-guard: PASS — new file uses timestamp format (no new legacy); stale file is not in .applied-migrations.json so deletion is allowed

verification.idempotent: All GRANT / ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES statements are safe to re-run

post_merge.action: Apply GRANT directly to Neon prod via MCP (migration runs on next Render deploy; prod needs it immediately)
